md3-practical.label: MD3 Practical
md3-practical.summary: Android-native clarity, predictable controls, and the lowest implementation risk.
md3-practical.bestFor: Choose this when consistency and speed matter more than a softer reading mood.
md3-practical.tradeoff: The reader and preference-heavy screens feel more utilitarian than the Cupertino-leaning pack.
notes: Use Material 3 components as the visible default.
Keep workflows direct and avoid decorative reader chrome.

reading-calm.label: Reading Calm
reading-calm.summary: Grouped settings, quiet reader chrome, and softer mobile-first navigation.
reading-calm.bestFor: Choose this when Hibiki should feel primarily like a long-session reading app.
reading-calm.tradeoff: Management-heavy surfaces may need explicit overrides to stay dense enough.
notes: Prefer grouped settings, large-title rhythm, and translucent reader/accessory chrome.
Keep dictionary results readable instead of input-focused.

adaptive-power.label: Adaptive Power
adaptive-power.summary: Dense workspaces, split panes, inspectors, and tablet or desktop readiness.
adaptive-power.bestFor: Choose this when dictionary management, creator mapping, tags, and diagnostics dominate.
adaptive-power.tradeoff: Casual mobile reading can feel too operational unless reader surfaces are overridden.
notes: Favor navigation rail/sidebar, split panes, inspectors, persistent previews, and compact shared components.
Keep mobile layouts usable by collapsing dense panels into sheets.

hibiki-balanced.label: Hibiki Balanced
hibiki-balanced.summary: Calm reader surfaces, dense management surfaces, and strict shared component tokens.
hibiki-balanced.bestFor: Choose this as the recommended baseline before making per-interface exceptions.
hibiki-balanced.tradeoff: It is less visually uniform than a pure A/B/C pack, so shared tokens must be strict.
notes: Reader stays calm; management surfaces stay dense.
Shared components use hybrid density so pages do not drift.
